prompt:
Our greenhouse gateway batches sensor readings and stores each batch as one capture blob; the same bytes later get parsed on the ingest server. I need the codec for that blob format in C17: two new files, recfile.h and recfile.c, stdlib headers only (stdint.h/stddef.h/string.h). It has to be explicit byte-at-a-time serialization -- the wire format is little-endian regardless of host, and struct memory layout must never touch the wire (no fwrite-ing structs, no memcpy of the whole record).

The header must define exactly this record type, these error codes, and this API:

    typedef struct {
        uint16_t sensor;   /* channel id */
        uint32_t at;       /* seconds since gateway boot */
        int32_t  value;    /* reading in centi-units, may be negative */
        uint8_t  flags;    /* bit0 = calibrated, rest reserved */
    } rf_reading;

    enum {
        RF_OK       = 0,
        RF_EARG     = -1,   /* bad pointers/capacities/counts */
        RF_ETRUNC   = -2,   /* buffer length does not fit the format */
        RF_EMAGIC   = -3,
        RF_EVERSION = -4,
        RF_ECRC     = -5
    };

    #define RF_MAX_RECORDS 4096

    size_t   rf_size(size_t n);
    uint16_t rf_crc16(const void *data, size_t len);
    long     rf_encode(const rf_reading *recs, size_t n, void *out, size_t cap);
    long     rf_decode(const void *buf, size_t len, rf_reading *out, size_t max);

Blob layout, byte-exact:

    offset 0..3   magic bytes 'S' 'R' '1' '\0'
    offset 4      version, currently 1
    offset 5      reserved, written as 0, ignored on read
    offset 6..7   record count, u16 LE
    then count records, 11 bytes each, in order:
                  u16 LE sensor | u32 LE at | i32 LE value (two's
                  complement) | u8 flags
    last 2 bytes  CRC-16 over EVERYTHING before them (header + records),
                  stored LE

CRC is CRC-16/CCITT-FALSE: polynomial 0x1021, initial value 0xFFFF, no bit reflection, no final xor, fed most-significant-bit first. Check value: the ASCII bytes of "123456789" give 0x29B1. rf_crc16 with len 0 returns 0xFFFF. An empty capture (count 0) is exactly these 10 bytes: 53 52 31 00 01 00 00 00 C3 A4.

Semantics:

- rf_size(n) = encoded byte length for n records (10 + 11*n).
- rf_encode writes the full blob including CRC, returns the byte count written. RF_EARG when: recs is NULL while n > 0, out is NULL, n > RF_MAX_RECORDS, or cap < rf_size(n). An exact-size cap is fine. Nothing is written on error.
- rf_decode validates strictly IN THIS ORDER so corrupt files give stable diagnostics: NULL buf -> RF_EARG; len < rf_size(0) -> RF_ETRUNC; magic -> RF_EMAGIC; version -> RF_EVERSION; len != rf_size(count) -> RF_ETRUNC (short OR long); CRC over the first len-2 bytes vs the stored trailer -> RF_ECRC; then count > max (or out NULL while count > 0) -> RF_EARG; finally fill out and return the count.
- Round-trip is exact for the full ranges: sensor 0..65535, at 0..4294967295, value INT32_MIN..INT32_MAX, flags 0..255.
- No globals, no allocation, no I/O in the codec.

`make test` is the acceptance gate; Makefile, mintest.h and test_recfile.c are fixed. The tests pin the empty and a two-record blob byte for byte, the CRC vectors above, and every reject path.
